reforms.description: Sweden has implemented the 'Industrial Leap' subsidy programme to accelerate the green industrial transition, supporting projects that reduce process emissions and promote strategic initiatives like hydrogen and battery production. Additionally, the 'Climate Leap' funding programme supports local and regional climate investments, focusing on projects such as electric vehicle charging infrastructure. A support scheme for the introduction of electric lorries, buses, and non-road mobility aims to further accelerate the transition to electrified heavy road transport.
reforms.component_name: Industrial Leap and Climate Leap Programmes
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These programmes are designed to foster innovation and investment in green technologies, which are expected to enhance productivity and support sustainable economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been officially launched and are currently in operation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent a significant structural change aimed at transitioning to a greener economy.
reforms.source_quote: The 'Industrial Leap' is a subsidy programme aiming to speed up the green industrial transition.

reforms.description: Sweden has introduced regulations requiring charging infrastructure for all parking slots in new buildings, along with grants covering up to 50% of the investment cost to support the transition to electric vehicles.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The investment in charging infrastructure is expected to facilitate the adoption of electric vehicles, thereby supporting environmental goals and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The regulations and grants are currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of regulations and grants represents a significant but narrower change in infrastructure policy.
reforms.source_quote: Sweden introduced regulations requiring charging infrastructure for all parking slots in new buildings in 2020.
